query: how do I set accessible color contrast for body text
notes: c24 is the canonical contrast-ratio explanation (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large, SC 1.4.3). c25 covers contrast-checking tools. c26 covers building a compliant palette. Single-concept query targeting color-contrast.

query: focus indicator requirements visible keyboard focus
notes: c39 is the SC 2.4.7 Focus Visible explanation. Tight single-concept query.

query: skip link bypass repeated navigation keyboard
notes: c40 covers SC 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks and the skip-navigation-link pattern. Single-concept.

query: alt text for images informative decorative functional
notes: c13 is the W3C image categorization explanation (informative, decorative, functional, complex). c14 gives good-vs-bad alt text examples. Single-concept.

query: ARIA live region status message dynamic content
notes: c85 covers SC 4.1.3 Status Messages and aria-live regions. Single-concept.

query: ARIA live region announcing form validation errors
notes: Multi-concept — c72 covers SC 3.3.1 Error Identification with aria-errormessage + aria-invalid; c85 covers the live-region announcement pattern. Retriever should surface BOTH for this query.

query: focus trapping and focus restoration for modal dialogs
notes: Multi-concept — c41 introduces the four focus-management behaviors for modals; c104 covers the accessible modal dialog pattern with the WAI-ARIA seven-behavior spec. Both are directly relevant.

query: VPAT Section 508 accessibility conformance report documentation
notes: Multi-concept — c96 explains the VPAT 2.5 format and ACR. c114 covers Section 508 federal ICT accessibility obligations. Together they answer the procurement/compliance question.

query: WCAG SC 1.4.3 contrast minimum requirements
notes: Structured SC ref. c24 is the direct 1.4.3 explanation (Contrast Minimum). c27 covers SC 1.4.1 (Use of Color) which is adjacent — included because the query sometimes conflates them; retriever should still put c24 first.

query: WCAG SC 2.4.7 focus visible requirement
notes: Structured SC ref. c39 is the canonical 2.4.7 explanation.

query: WCAG SC 3.3.2 labels and instructions for form fields
notes: Structured SC ref. c70 is the SC 3.3.2 explanation with label-element, for-attribute, aria-describedby, fieldset.

query: screen reader testing accessibility assistive technology
notes: Synonym-recall query — the target chunk talks about keyboard-and-screen-reader testing with NVDA/VoiceOver. Tests that 'screen reader' and 'assistive technology' both surface c94 even though the chunk mixes the two phrasings.

query: keyboard trap focus not escapable
notes: Synonym-recall — 'keyboard trap' is the canonical WCAG term (SC 2.1.2). 'focus not escapable' is the functional synonym users might type. c37 is the SC 2.1.1 + 2.1.2 explanation.

query: accessible dialog element modal keyboard behavior
notes: Synonym-recall — HTML <dialog> element vs the generic 'modal' pattern. Both chunks describe the same accessibility requirements from different angles.

query: how do I test my site with NVDA screen reader
notes: Procedural / how-to. c94 explains keyboard and screen-reader testing techniques including NVDA specifically.

query: running automated accessibility tests with axe-core in CI
notes: Procedural — c92 explains how axe-core works (DOM + accessibility tree traversal). c93 gives a Lighthouse-CI config example for CI integration.

query: how do I conduct a WCAG-EM accessibility audit
notes: Procedural — c123 is the canonical WCAG-EM five-step methodology explanation.

query: apply POUR principles to an ecommerce checkout page
notes: Bloom-targeted (Apply). c3 explains the four POUR principles; c1 is the screen-reader navigation exercise that applies POUR to real sites — closest 'apply' content. No chunk specifically walks through ecommerce, so the retriever should surface principle + application chunks together.

query: build an accessible tab component with ARIA roles and keyboard
notes: Bloom-targeted (Create). c101 is the Week-10 tab-component build exercise (WAI-ARIA APG tab pattern). c105 is the explanatory 'Accessible Tabs Pattern' that c101 references.

query: do all images need alt text or do some use empty alt
notes: Misconception-driven — common learner misconception is that every needs descriptive alt. c13 explicitly distinguishes informative (descriptive alt), decorative (alt=''), and functional (action-describing alt) categories.
